title: Rigor evaluation manifest v1
description: A versioned, contamination-aware description of the dogfooding evaluation task set. It is an input document, never an evidence artifact; the CLI never invokes a model on its behalf. `split` is the only barrier between calibration fitting and the holdout set: holdout tasks must never be used to fit thresholds or profiles.

owner.description: The recurring calibration owner responsible for reviewing evaluation reports and proposals.

reviewInterval.description: A concrete recurring review interval, for example "every release" or "monthly".

expansionPolicy.description: A statement of how the initial 10-20 task set expands toward 30-100 tasks without contaminating the holdout set.

fixtureRef.description: Optional repository-relative reference to an isolated worktree or fixture used for a documented cross-model comparison. It is never an absolute path and is never executed by the CLI.

crossModelComparison.description: Marks a task selected for a documented, human-approved cross-model comparison. It records process intent only; the CLI performs no live multi-model run.
